Issues: Lack of context regarding intellectual property ownership (IP rights). If the creator does not own the full rights to the image, selling it can lead to legal issues.

The stylized aesthetic, while commercially appealing, relies heavily on the specific artistic style. If the goal is to sell it as a unique piece, the style needs to be extremely distinct or tied to a specific, licensed character/brand.

Commercial appeal is dependent on the target market. While trendy styles attract buyers, generic illustrations have lower resale value unless they are extremely high quality or tie into a strong niche.
